answer_text: We refer the Member to AQW 26224/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) any engagement his Department has had with the South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust (SEHSCT) following its decision to cancel its summer schemes in Lisburn and Downpatrick; and (ii) any financial support his Department can offer to the SEHSCT to enable these summer schemes to be reinstated.
answer_text: The Department has not had any engagement with South Eastern Health and Social Care Trust following its decision to cancel its summer schemes in Lisburn and Downpatrick. It is understood, given the challenging financial position of the Trust, and the requirement to minimise all discretionary areas of spend there was no request to the Department to provide funding to sustain a non-core service.The Department is not in a position currently to offer financial support.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to detail (i) any engagement they had during the 2024-25 financial year in relation to the potential development of the Maze Long Kesh site; and (ii) a timescale for any development.
answer_text: We met with the Board of the Maze/Long Kesh Development Corporation (MLKDC) on 10 July 2024 and the Board met the TEO Permanent Secretary in September 2024 and in February 2025. We have since received advice from officials on the options for the Board and on the immediate priorities for the site. We have also had correspondence from MLKDC in respect of the Investment Strategy.Advice on the options is under consideration, but no decisions have been made yet on the future of the site. Ministers recognise the immense economic, historical and reconciliation potential of the site and remain committed to working with the Board to maximise the potential of the site for the benefit of all.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any budget Sport NI has in place for the hosting of training camps for Northern Ireland athletes prior to next year's Commonwealth Games.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Sport NI.I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:“There are two areas within Commonwealth Games Northern Ireland (CGNI) current budget that is directed towards camps for Commonwealth Games athletes prior to the games for this period:Team NI Hub & Support Camp – Support camp that will be based in Glasgow ahead of the event.Combined Camp – Yearly high-performance camp held in NI aimed at bringing CGNI athletes and support personnel together.Please see table 1 below for detail.Table 1: Funding for CGNI CampsSub Budget HeadingYear 1Year 2Year 3Year 4Total2023-242024-252025-262026-27Team NI Hub & Support Camp010,00020,00020,00050,000Futures – Combined Camp014,17015,00015,00044,170£10,000 has been spent to secure accommodation in 2024/25 for the Team NI Hub & Support Camp; and £14,170 spent for a coach/practitioner event held in Belfast in January 2025.Year 3 and Year 4 funding is pending approval by the Sport NI Board. At present, no training camps have been planned or scheduled. Sport NI will be requesting a project plan from CGNI now that funding for 2025/26 and 2026/27 has passed the Committee stage and is proceeding to Sport NI Board.For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact Sport NI directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make the best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to outline how the Northern Ireland Prison Service is managing the high prison population, including prisoners with complex needs.
answer_text: ORAL QUESTION No 13 - AQO 1927/22-27ANSWEROn 1 April 2022, the total prison population in Northern Ireland was 1,552.  On 1 April 2025 the population had increased by over 26% to 1,964.At Maghaberry Prison, as a result of the rise in population, the Prison Service has re-opened two out of three square houses (Foyle and Erne).  This is accommodation which closed when Davis House, the most modern accommodation, was opened.  The last remaining house at Maghaberry has been prepared for occupation and will be required if the population trend does not change.The Prison Service is also closely monitoring and managing the balance of the population between Maghaberry and Magilligan Prisons and maximising the safe use of cell sharing where possible.  The continued priority is to deliver the best prison regimes and services possible within available resources.  From a resource perspective 69 new entrant prison officers commenced training on 31 March and will be deployed on the 19 June if they successfully complete the recruit assessment course. It is of course the case that many people coming into custody have complex needs.  Drug and alcohol addiction, poor levels of education, additional educational needs and poor physical and mental health are just some of the societal issues concentrated in our prisons.  The Prison Service is responding to that challenge working across government and in partnership with private, statutory, voluntary and community sector organisations to provide the support needed to address these complex needs.It is also the case that much more could be achieved if there was collective focus on prevention in the community, rather than the justice system having to take responsibility when earlier opportunities have been missed, or when provision was not effective or inadequate.It is simply unsustainable to say that when someone comes into the custody that addressing their needs and risks becomes solely a criminal justice issue.  A collective cross-departmental response to these challenges is required.

To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any proactive measures Sport NI has undertaken to make efficiencies since April 2022.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Sport NI.		I have sought the requested information from the Chief Executive, who has advised me as follows:	“Sport NI does not directly monitor and report details of financial savings arising from efficiencies. Sport NI’s focus is on living within the resource and capital budgets allocated to us by the Department, which have remained flat for the last 2 years, against a background of inflationary cost pressures. If efficiency savings are made in year, any such money is spent on sport development to maximise the impact of the budget allocated to Sport NI.”For any future queries relating to operational matters, you may wish to contact Sport NI directly.  Making direct contact with the ALB will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the development of proposals to realign the junction of the A26 Glenavy Road and Station Road, Moira, with particular reference to traffic from the Ballinderry direction turning right to access Moira Train Station, where the development of the new park and ride facility is due to start.
answer_text: My Department has looked at a number of options to provide a right turn lane from the A26 Glenavy Road into Station Road, Moira. Unfortunately a viable scheme has not yet been identified and as such the junction layout cannot be changed at this time.Officials will keep the situation under review together with other similar locations on the highway network.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether there are any circumstances in Northern Ireland that would mean the Renewable Electricity Support Scheme should start at a different level from the 0.5 megawatts as used in the equivalent scheme in the Republic of Ireland.
answer_text: Collaboration with wider stakeholders, including industry representatives, has been integral to the development of the Renewable Electricity Support Scheme. In particular, the lower capacity threshold for inclusion in the scheme will be tailored to suit the unique requirements of the power market in the north of Ireland. My Department is assessing the impact of the north’s existing large fleet of distributed generation and the contracts and security of supply risk for delivery partners posed by the management of numerous small generators. I recognise the ongoing contribution of distributed renewable electricity projects in the north, and options for a viable route to market continue to be assessed by my Department.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail any engagement his Department has had with the Department for the Economy to seek an increase in the student finance provision for those undertaking allied health professional degree courses.
answer_text: Officials from the Workforce Policy Directorate in my Department met with Department for the Economy officials on 8 November 2023. Discussions included the future possibility of introducing changes to the package of support for those undertaking allied health professional degree courses. There has been no further engagement on this matter, however my officials will work closely with the Department for Economy should any changes be proposed to the current arrangements.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any plans his Department has to review student finance provision for students undertaking allied health professional degree courses, in light of the 20 per cent increase in other degree course areas.
answer_text: The recent announcement of a 20 per cent increase in Maintenance Loan support applies to all eligible full-time undergraduate students for academic year 2025/26, including eligible allied health professional students. Specific student financial support for students undertaking allied health professional degree courses is a policy matter for the Department of Health.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to outline the impact of changes to the licensing scheme for the Duke of Edinburgh's Award and Gaisce - The President's Award on local schools and youth settings.
answer_text: The Duke of Edinburgh Northern Ireland office has notified the Education Authority (EA), that local schools and some voluntary youth organisations are not permitted to operate under the terms of the current Education Authority Operating Centre Licence. Settings affected have been invited to become a ‘Directly Licensed Centre.’ These settings have been assured that funding will come directly from the Duke of Edinburgh Northern Ireland office for the initial license fee and any other associated costs to ensure that a transition to a Directly Licensed Centre is initially cost neutral.Forty-nine settings received notification that they could no longer remain under the current Education Authority Operating Centre Licence of which twenty-nine have progressed to become a Directly Licenced Centre or moved to another suitable Operating Authority. For settings which have not yet progressed to a Directly Licenced Centre licence Duke of Edinburgh Northern Ireland have amended their online system to ensure that young people can continue their Award programme.  To allow expeditions suitable licensing agreements are expected to be in place before the end of this summer.  If any setting decides not to continue, young people will be supported to find an alternative centre.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to confirm the date on which he wrote to the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland to ask for an update on the UK Government's view of the Casement Park redevelopment.
answer_text: 6 January 2025.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to provide an update on progress to redevelop Casement Park.

To ask the Minister of Education to clarify the position of voluntary organisations following notification from the Duke of Edinburgh NI Office to the Education Authority that they are not permitted to operate under the current Education Authority Operating Centre Licence.
answer_text: The DofE Northern Ireland office has notified the Education Authority (EA), that Voluntary Grammar Schools, Grant Maintained Integrated Schools, Independent Schools and some voluntary youth organisations are not permitted to operate under the terms of the current EA Operating Centre Licence. DofE Northern Ireland have written to affected schools and voluntary youth organisations inviting them to become a ‘Directly Licensed Centre’. I understand DofE Northern Ireland have assured affected settings that they have secured funding for the cost of the licence fee and any other associated costs (staff training, or additional insurance premiums) to ensure that a transition to a Directly Licensed Centre is initially cost neutral for schools and voluntary youth organisations and that there is a longer-term plan for ongoing licence fee support.  The funding for the initial license fee will come from DofE NI directly.

answer_text: The DofE Northern Ireland office has notified the Education Authority (EA), that Voluntary Grammar Schools, Grant Maintained Integrated Schools, Independent Schools and some voluntary youth organisations are not permitted to operate under the terms of the current EA Operating Centre Licence. The funding for the initial license fee will come from DofE NI directly.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the educational and training provision for prisoners.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount that is currently invested in Go Succeed by the UK Shared Prosperity Fund.
answer_text: The UK Shared Prosperity Fund (SPF) is implemented directly in the north of Ireland by the British Government’s Ministry for Housing, Communities & Local Government (MHCLG). Questions on the SPF should be directed to that department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the amount of the UK Shared Prosperity Fund that is spent on client-facing activity in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The UK Shared Prosperity Fund (SPF) is implemented directly in the north of Ireland by the British Government’s Ministry for Housing, Communities & Local Government (MHCLG).Questions on the SPF should be directed to that department.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will consider amending the Department’s policy in relation to the provision of street lighting to include public roads, regardless of classification, going to and from railway stations.
answer_text: I am conscious that street lighting can contribute significantly to feelings of personal security and safety, particularly for women travelling at night. This is an important consideration when officials are developing proposals to improve pedestrian and cycling facilities to key destinations with the aim of encouraging modal shift, such as routes to railway halts.However, the desire for lighting has to be balanced against environmental considerations, our desire to reduce the carbon footprint, electricity costs associated with our infrastructure, and limited budgets. Decisions on provision or removal of lighting are made taking account of these considerations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will consider amending the Department’s policy in relation to the provision of street lighting to include public roads, regardless of classification, going to and from park and ride facilities.
answer_text: I am conscious that street lighting can contribute significantly to feelings of personal security and safety, particularly for women travelling at night. This is an important consideration when officials are developing proposals to improve pedestrian and cycling facilities to key destinations with the aim of encouraging modal shift, such as routes to Park & Ride sites near towns. However, the desire for lighting has to be balanced against environmental considerations, our desire to reduce the carbon footprint and electricity costs associated with our infrastructure, and limited budgets. Decisions on provision or removal of lighting are made taking account of these considerations.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when the reflective road studs on the A1 between Sprucefield and the border at Meigh were last (i) reviewed; and (ii) improved.
answer_text: The A1 Dual Carriageway from Sprucefield to the border is maintained on behalf of my Department by Amey Roads (NI) under the Design, Build, Finance and Operate package 2 contract.The DBFO contractor inspects road studs on an annual basis, with the last inspection carried out during August 2024. A programme of repairs is underway and will be completed early 2025 subject to favourable weather conditions.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether the plans for an upgrade to the A1 will include improved lighting.
answer_text: The A1 Junctions Phase 2 scheme will include for new and improved lighting at a number of strategic locations along the route in line with my Department’s policy. Additional lighting will be included at the four new compact grade separated junctions and improved lighting, using LED technologies, will also be provided at a number of existing minor road junctions connecting to the A1, which are currently illuminated and are to be upgraded as part of the proposed project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the review of pay and conditions for further education lecturers, including a timeline on the completion of the review.
answer_text: In April 2024 I approved a pay remit business case submitted by the Further Education Colleges, as employers, for lecturers that aligned to the pay settlement provided to teachers in the North of Ireland for 2023/24. Conditions agreed between the employers and lecturing trade unions within the pay agreement included a commitment to teacher pay scale parity to be informed through an independent examination of the pay, all terms (including addressing policy review) and conditions for lecturers; develop an industrial relations model that will transform and modernise the existing pay, reward, and conditions of service for all academic staff within regional FE Colleges; and for key deliverables and timescales to be agreed.		Due to the cessation of the College Employers’ Forum, I commissioned an independent review on the collaborative working arrangements between the Further Education Colleges in respect of staff terms and conditions, including pay.  I am currently reviewing the final draft of this collaboration review, with a view to share with College Governing Body Chairs and Principals/Chief Executives. This will enable a structured forum to be formed within the sector to address industrial relations and deliver the conditions specified within the 2023/24 pay offer for lecturers.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure, pursuant to AQW 6562/22-27, to detail any proposals that have been developed to improve the junction of the A26 Glenavy Road and Station Road, Moira, with particular reference to traffic from the Ballinderry direction turning right to access the park and ride facility at Moira Train Station.
answer_text: My Department has carried out a feasibility study on possible improvements to the Glenavy Road / Station Road / Magheramesk Lane junction, to provide a right turn lane into Station Road.  This work derived from an earlier Route Improvement Study of the A26 Glenavy Road and is separate from the traffic impact assessment undertaken as part of the planning application for a Park & Ride site at Moira train station.  While this has developed a range of preliminary options, which range in size, design standards and cost, the current side road layout does not easily lend itself to the provision of right turn lane into Station Road while still maintaining the current facilities and catering for all traffic movements.  As such, my Department will continue to develop these proposals.A Transport Assessment Form for the Moira Park and Ride development was provided and modelling of the impact of the junctions at both ends of Station Road was carried out by the applicant’s agent at the request of my department. This showed that there was less than 5% intensification of the A26 Glenavy Road/Maghreamesk Lane junction. This junction therefore did not warrant improvement as part of the development.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure why three carriage trains, with limited capacity, are continuing to be used on the Portadown line during peak times, despite assurances from Translink that six carriage trains would be in place.
answer_text: This issue is an operational matter for Translink.  I have sought the requested information and can confirm that NI Railways operate a total of 16 services in the Belfast direction prior to 0900 hours from Monday through to Friday. Out of these, four services are operated by trains with 6 or 8 carriages, the remaining 12 services are operated by 3 carriages.  The current railway timetable has over 50 services that utilise 6 or 8 car trains operational on the Portadown corridor each day, compared with 20 before the Belfast Grand Station opened. All the available NI Railways fleet are in service during the peak periods.  For any future queries relating to operational matters, you should contact Translink directly at aqw.request@translink.co.uk.  Making direct contact with Translink will ensure that you receive the information in the shortest possible timeframe and make best use of limited public resources.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will directly engage with the family to purchase the final piece of land required to complete the B101 North Lisburn feeder road, Pond Park Link, in order to progress this issue.
answer_text: The North Lisburn Feeder Road is a developer led scheme associated with residential development in north Lisburn. There is an Article 122 Agreement in place between the North Lisburn Development Consortium (NLDC) and my Department to facilitate the acquisition of the final piece of land required, by using the Department’s vesting powers, if necessary, to complete the scheme between the B101 at Lady Wallace Avenue joining the Pond Park Road just north of Old Park Road. As this is a developer led project, my Department would not engage / negotiate with the  landowner.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a timeline on the completion of the park and ride facility at Lisburn West rail halt.
answer_text: The proposed Lisburn West rail halt and associated park and ride facilities is at the preliminary stages of development. A Strategic Outline Case for the project is due to be submitted to my Department by summer 2025. Construction of the new halt could start in 2027, and be operational by the end of 2028, subject to the requisite approvals and availability of funding.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for a timeline on the completion of the enhanced park and ride facility at Moira Train Station.
answer_text: In October 2024, my Department issued a letter of offer for £6.6m to Translink to provide 466 new car parking spaces with safe and convenient links to the Moira Train Station. The project will also deliver 23 new disabled parking spaces, along with secure cycle parking, CCTV and lighting. The project is expected start in Spring 2025 and be completed by Summer 2026.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the current figure available from his Department for the Casement Park project, given the £62.5 million figure originally set aside in 2011.
answer_text: NI Executive funding of £62.5m was committed for the redevelopment of Casement Park in 2011 and this remains available to the Department for Communities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any work his Department has carried out on the likely impact on trade, particularly in packaged goods, of the General Product Safety Regulation in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: Product safety is a reserved matter for the British Government. The Department for Business and Trade holds competence for the General Product Safety Regulation. 		It is not possible to provide detailed analysis on matters outside the department’s official responsibilities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans his Department has to extend the on-street residents only parking zones, with particular reference to Lagan Valley.
answer_text: My Department has recently completed a review of residents’ parking schemes,  the report has been shared with the Committee for Infrastructure and it is planned that it will be made public on 29 November 2024.  The findings of the review will be used to establish the policy for such schemes going forward, including those in Lagan Valley.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the average response time from each Roads Division to reports received from elected representatives in the last three years.
answer_text: My Department does not maintain statistics in the format requested.Following reports from the public or public representative of potholes it may be necessary for my officials to carry out a site investigation to enable a decision on whether the pothole meets our intervention criteria and the creation of an instructed surface defect record.  My Department receives a high volume of surface defect related enquiries, and regrettably it is not possible to investigate all enquiries immediately. It is likely that some faults will not be investigated until the next routine inspection of the area.If, after investigation, it is found that the reported pothole requires action, our current standards specify a range of response times for the repair of defects, depending on factors such as their severity and the volume of traffic on the road. They range from one calendar day for the most serious defects, to periods of five working days and four weeks for less serious defects.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether his Department has plans to review the current ApprenticeshipsNI programme to extend the support it provides to other forms of apprenticeship such as workplace or training provider-led schemes.
answer_text: The ApprenticeshipsNI programme supports the off-the-job training elements of an apprenticeship at Levels 2 and 3. Apprentices are employees and paid directly by their employer, with the directed training element provided by 20 contracted private training organisations, the six Further Education Colleges and the College of Agriculture, Food and Rural Enterprise (CAFRE). My Department spends over £25m per year on funding the off the job element of an apprenticeship under the ApprenticeshipsNI programme.0ver 6,600 people started a Level 2 or Level 3 apprenticeship in the 2023/24 academic year to end April 2024, a 30% increase on 2022/23. As of 30 April 2024, there were 10,433 apprentices enrolled on the ApprenticeshipsNI Programme. To ensure our apprenticeship provision aligns with the changing needs of our economy, Sectoral Partnerships have been established across a number of economic sectors. They bring employers, qualification experts and policy advisors together with the aim of ensuring that apprenticeships training and qualifications meet the needs of industry. Whilst there are no plans to extend the ApprenticeshipsNI programme to non-employees, my Department is working on expanding apprenticeship opportunities to more people.  Women, people with disabilities, and people from disadvantaged areas are currently underrepresented in the apprenticeship system. The recently launched Apprenticeship Inclusion Challenge Fund is intended to drive collaboration across the education, business and voluntary and community sectors to develop and deliver innovative solutions to these inclusion challenges.My Department offers alternative programmes, such as Skills for Life and Work and Traineeships, which are designed to help young people get the skills and experience needed to obtain future employment.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to outline the budget required for the Women's Health Action Plan.

To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what input his Department will have into the development of any aviation strategy agreed by the Executive, relating to road and rail access, further to York Aviation's Air Connectivity to Support Inbound Tourism and Business Growth report submitted to the Department for the Economy.

To ask the Minister for Communities to outline (i) how many Northern Ireland Football League Clubs are based in local council-owned facilities; and (ii) of those, how many are leased on long-term rental agreements from their local council.
answer_text: My Department does not hold verified information about the ownership or lease agreements of Northern Ireland Football League Clubs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the revised street lighting policy, RSPPG E072, applicable from 19 August 2015, specifically with reference as to whether it is being correctly implemented and providing value for money.
answer_text: RSPPG E072 details my Department’s policy for the provision of street lighting, which was established in 1993 and can be accessed via the following link.Street lighting RSPPG E072 | Department for Infrastructure (infrastructure-ni.gov.uk)The document is subject to continual informal review. It was formally reviewed in August 2015 when my Officials took the opportunity to amalgamate several other policy guides into this document. The details can be found in the review section on page 23 of the document. No revisions were made to the policy for the provision of street lighting at this time.I can assure the member that this policy is correctly implemented and provides value for money, ensuring that the limited resources available for the provision of street lighting are directed to those areas where they can provide the maximum benefit to the public, particularly where road or infrastructure safety is concerned.It also serves to balance the demand for more rural lighting against the unwelcome effects of increased urbanisation of the countryside and the environmental impact of night sky light pollution on people, wildlife and fauna.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how often issues have been reported, particularly in rural areas, as a result of street lights being removed or permanently switched off since the implementation of RSPPG E072 in August 2015.
answer_text: Since August 2015, my Department has received 243 enquiries regarding the removal of street lights.I can also confirm that no street lights have been permanently switched off.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any actions carried out by her Department further to the publication of the Law Commission’s Report on Land Law - NILC 8 in 2010 and its recommendation that the two pieces of draft legislation, Land Law Reform and Ground Rents, should be implemented in full.
answer_text: This area of policy currently falls to a small and busy team which has a broad civil law reform remit. Priorities for the remainder of this mandate include delivering reform through a Marriage and Civil Partnership Bill, exploring policy development in relation to the issue of no-fault divorce as well as providing advice and direction across a range of other diverse policy areas which include land law.Initial work was commenced in relation to land law reform by the Department some years ago following the publication of the Law Commission report. Following discussions both inside the Department and with the Office of Legislative Counsel it was concluded that implementation of the detailed and complex recommendations of the Law Commission’s report would require a fully-funded project team involving both subject-matter and legislative process experts. At that time, as now, that expertise and resource was not available in the Department.While there are aspects of the report that may be desirable in terms of legislative reform, further consideration of the Law Commission recommendations will be dependent on future resource and competing priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) why replacement LED lights on NIE Networks poles have been removed in areas of Lagan Valley when they were not at the end of their serviceable life; (ii) the cost of the replacement LED lights; and (iii) the cost of the original lights.
answer_text: When a street lighting system in an area reaches the end of its serviceable life, a survey is carried out of the location affected and replacement lighting is installed only in those areas which meet my Department’s policy for the provision of road lighting. At that stage, any lights located in areas which do not meet this policy, including any individual LED units that may have been erected previously during routine maintenance, are removed.  Any LED units removed, that are still in serviceable condition, will be utilised elsewhere.This policy helps ensure replacement lighting is provided where it is most needed as well as reducing future maintenance and energy costs and environmental impacts.The costs to install a LED street lighting luminaire on a network operators wood pole range from £300-£400, depending on the type of light and the location.My Department does not hold any information on the cost of the original lights.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the locations where street lights have been (i) removed; and (ii) permanently turned off since 19 August 2015.
answer_text: As part of its ongoing capital replacement programme to replace street lights that have reached the end of their lives, my Department removes and replaces many street lights each year, depending on the available funding.In considering those lighting installations which are deemed to have reached the end of their serviceable life and can no longer be maintained, a survey is carried out of the location and lighting is replaced only in those adopted areas which meet my Department’s policy for the provision of lighting. This can result in the removal and non-replacement of lights in areas such as back pathways, unadopted roads and footways, or rural areas with insufficient density of housing. These lights are removed for safety reasons as they can no longer be maintained, there are no instances where lights have been permanently turned off.This policy helps ensure that the limited resources available for the provision of street lighting are directed at those areas where they can provide the maximum benefit to the public, particularly where road or infrastructure safety is concernedWhilst my Department keeps detailed records of the number of street lights across the network, details of lights which have been removed and not replaced are not recorded separately and would not be easily attained.  To attempt to provide details of these removals would require an extensive exercise to be conducted across the department which might only be expected to produce data with a limited degree of exactitude, and this exercise itself would very likely involve disproportionate costs.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for his assessment of the risk to the budget of Sport NI for grassroots and elite sports, over the remaining Assembly mandate.
answer_text: I am concerned that the constraints on my Department’s budget mean that I do not have the funding I would wish to have to promote sport, or indeed to meet the demand for housing, arts, heritage and other functions of my Department.The current Sport NI budget helps to cover essential costs for the organisation including running costs for the Sports Institute and the Tollymore National Outdoor Centre as well as providing grant funding to several organisations across the sector such as Disability Sport NI and Special Olympics Ulster.As is the case for all publicly funded bodies, budgets have reduced over the last ten years and organisations have had to adapt to absorb funding reductions as well as inflationary pressures. While Sport NI continues to deliver efficiencies and consider different methods of service delivery to live within its budgets, there is no doubt that this becomes more difficult as pressures increase.Lottery funding is also distributed by Sport NI to fund sport in Northern Ireland, and it remains to be seen how the quantum of this funding may change over the next few years after the recent change in National Lottery licence holder to Allwyn.I will continue to push for additional funding to enable not only the maintenance of current levels of funding, but to increase the support we give to sport and other areas which fall under the responsibility of my Department.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the priorities of the Executive Insight and Strategy Unit.
answer_text: The Executive Insight and Strategy Unit (formerly known as the Economic Policy Unit) was formed in 2023 to develop a stronger understanding of our economic performance and international competitiveness, and to support policy development in the Department.  The unit has developed relevant socio-economic research on the Measuring the Missions, Competitiveness and modelling the impact of inclusion, while working with colleagues across the Executive and external stakeholders.  Since 5 August 2024, the Unit has also been responsible for providing Ministerial briefing and the economic appraisal of strategically important business cases.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs what engagement he has had with the UK Government on the need for financial support for agriculture.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail any plans he has to improve transport access to Lisburn city centre.
answer_text: I can advise that my Department is currently preparing the Eastern Transport Plan 2035 (ETP). This is a new transport plan which will set the framework for making transport policy and investment decisions up until 2035. The ETP covers five council areas including Lisburn and Castlereagh City Council (LCCC).The ETP will support the preparation of the Local Development Plan (LDP) for LCCC and therefore the consideration of improved transport access to Lisburn city centre will be undertaken in line with the LCCC LDP timetable.In addition, officials are currently finalising a Northern Ireland wide Active Travel Delivery Plan.This includes an update of our policies and design guidance for active travel. It also includes active travel network plans for our larger towns and cities, including Lisburn and a methodology for prioritising pedestrian and cycling improvements in smaller towns and villages. The aim is to consult with the public on this plan later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities to detail any opportunities his Department has identified to improve arts provision in Lisburn city centre.
answer_text: In 2024-25 the Arts Council of Northern Ireland has provided £47k of lottery funding to the R-Space Gallery in Lisburn. The gallery aims to develop and deliver a high-quality arts programme that increases understanding, appreciation, practice, access and enjoyment of contemporary visual arts, crafts and other related arts activities. The programme for the year ahead will have:Six exhibitions by local, national and international artists/makers lasting 4/5 weeks each; and.An accompanying programme of relevant and accessible learning activities including workshops and artist talks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education to detail any plans his Department has to ensure school transport arrangements begin from when the school term starts, and not the arbitrary date of 1 September.
answer_text: I should explain that my Department is responsible for the Home to School Transport Policy and the Education Authority (EA) for the operation and delivery of school transport. The EA has no plans to make changes to the operational transport arrangements to accommodate schools who choose to commence the new school year before the 1st of September. The school transport service provides transport assistance to over 90,000 pupils each day and it is not logistically feasible to accommodate variable school start dates or the phasing-in arrangements that schools may put in place. Individual schools are responsible for determining their own calendar, however the EA writes to each school in February/March to advise them of the non-operational dates for the following academic year and that home to school transport provision does not commence until 1st September. Schools are informed by the EA that if they decide to open on a non-operational date, they must make parents aware that home to school transport may not be accessible. Further information on non-operational dates can be found on the EA website at Non-operational Dates 2024/25 | Education Authority Northern Ireland (eani.org.uk) I hope that you find this information helpful.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail any additional measures his Department can take to protect individuals from rogue traders who are exploiting existing loopholes.
answer_text: My Department’s Trading Standards Service (TSS), as a consumer protection regulator, can investigate complaints alleging unfair trading practices and can investigate breaches of consumer protection legislation, taking appropriate enforcement action, as necessary. I would recommend that any consumer who has concerns that a trader is treating them unfairly contact the TSS Consumerline service to obtain appropriate advice, relevant to their specific experiences.  Queries can be made to Consumerline via Freephone 0300 123 62 62 or by email to Consumerline@economy-ni.gov.uk.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will review (i) road markings; (ii) signage; and (iii) other safety issues for the entry points to rural villages, particularly in reference to Diamond Road, Dromore, in order to improve road users' awareness and the safety of pedestrians.
answer_text: My Department erected 30mph speed limits at villages which meet our policy ‘Setting Local Speed Limits in Northern Ireland’. Where necessary, village gateway treatments have been added in addition to the 30mph speed limit signs which include roundels and gateway signs.These signs and road markings are maintained by my Department and renewed when necessary. My officials undertake reviews of safety on all roads when assessing the injury collision data received from the PSNI.A review of PSNI collision data recorded on the Diamond Road, Dromore shows that this stretch of road has a reasonable safety record, and given the need for my Department to target its constrained funding where it will be most effective at providing road safety benefits, there are currently no plans to add to the existing rural entry point features at this location.  However, I have asked my officials to assess the current signs and line markings and add to the schedule of works if necessary.Any concerns about driver behaviour such as speeding, or parking which causes an obstruction to other road (and footway) users, or other inconsiderate behaviour should be directed to the police who have the powers of enforcement in that regard. They can be contacted on their non-emergency number, telephone 101.  One of the best ways to ensure safety on our roads and fulfil our long-term goal of eliminating death and serious injury by 2050, is by changing road user behaviour. As road users we all have a personal responsibility to behave in a way that keeps ourselves and others safe. The sad reality is that if we do not change our own attitudes when we are using the roads, our death toll will continue to increase.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the findings of any rural impact assessment that was carried out as part of the new street lighting policy.
answer_text: I can inform the member that my Department’s policy for the provision of street lighting has been in place since 1993 and has not changed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether he plans to introduce legislation to protect private apartment owners from disproportionate increases in management company fees.
answer_text: Management companies must act in accordance with existing relevant companies legislation, which falls outside the remit of my Department.However, this is an issue that also relates to substantive land law and is one that I am keen to see some progress in developing policy proposals. I am currently exploring with officials how to secure the appropriate expertise in what is a complex area. Further progress will depend on that and the competing priorities over the remainder of this mandate.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities whether he will introduce funding for the installation of energy efficiency and decarbonisation measures for low-income families in domestic properties.
To ask the Minister of Justice to provide an update on a review of the Northern Ireland Policing Board.
answer_text: ORAL QUESTION No - AQO 748/22-27			ANSWER Following my re-appointment as Justice Minister in February 24, I agreed with the previous Policing Board Chair that I (my department) would commission an independent review into the Northern Ireland Policing Board.  Since the original announcement by the Board, which was made in the immediate aftermath of the resignation of the former Chief Constable, the PSNI has had significant changes to its senior team and the Policing Board has new independent and political members, including a new Chair and vice Chair. As it was appropriate that such changes were given a reasonable period to settle in, I considered that it would not have been appropriate to rush the launch of a review.During this period, my officials have considered carefully the scope and terms of reference for the review in the context of subsequent changes within both PSNI and the Policing Board.I expect to finalise and agree the terms of reference for this review imminently and commit to sharing them with my Executive colleagues, the Justice Committee, the Northen Ireland Policing Board, and the PSNI.My officials are continuing to engage with suitably qualified individuals to secure a panel to take this work forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what action he is taking to put a weight limit in place to restrict heavy goods vehicle traffic through Hillsborough village.
answer_text: I am acutely aware of the desire of Royal Hillsborough residents to see a reduction in the volume of HGV traffic going through the village. Officials in my department have commissioned an assessment of alternative routes for HGV traffic to inform the relative merits of the weight limit proposal, in particular regarding the impacts on road safety both within the village and on the surrounding road network.The assessment is expected for completion in Autumn 2024.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what proposals he will bring forward by the end of the current Assembly session to improve traffic management in Hillsborough village.
answer_text: I am acutely aware of the desire of Royal Hillsborough residents to see a reduction in the volume of HGV traffic going through the village. Officials in my department have commissioned an assessment of alternative routes for HGV traffic to inform the relative merits of the weight limit proposal, in particular regarding the impacts on road safety both within the village and on the surrounding road network.The assessment is expected for completion in Autumn 2024 and I am unable to provide details of any proposed traffic management improvements within the village until the assessment is completed and my officials have had time to consider its contents.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether he will meet the Hillsborough Community Group to discuss a weight limit for heavy goods vehicle traffic in Hillsborough village.
answer_text: I am acutely aware of the desire of Royal Hillsborough residents to see a reduction in the volume of HGV traffic going through the village.My officials have previously met with members of the Hillsborough Community Group to discuss the weight limit proposal.  Following the most recent meeting, officials have commissioned an assessment of alternative routes for HGV traffic to inform the relative merits of the weight limit proposal, in particular regarding the impacts on road safety both within the village and on the surrounding road network.The assessment is expected for completion in Autumn 2024. Once my officials have had time to review and consider the report findings a further meeting will be arranged with the Hillsborough Community Group.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure what consideration he has given to putting a weight limit in place for heavy goods vehicle traffic in Hillsborough village.
answer_text: I am acutely aware of the desire of Royal Hillsborough residents to see a reduction in the volume of HGV traffic going through the village. Officials in my department have commissioned an assessment of alternative routes for HGV traffic to inform the relative merits of the weight limit proposal, in particular regarding the impacts on road safety both within the village and on the surrounding road network.The assessment is expected for completion in Autumn 2024.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister to outline what work their officials are undertaking in relation to the implementation of the findings of the High Street Task Force report on Delivering a 21st Century High Street.
answer_text: We recognise the important role High Streets play in our society in more ways than one. They drive the economy and also create shared spaces where society thrives.We are currently considering a submission from officials regarding the High Street Taskforce Report and appropriate next steps in implementing its recommendations.

question_text: To ask the Assembly Commission for its assessment of the security provision for Members and staff working alone in constituency offices.
To ask the Minister of Justice for an update on the implementation of the Post Office (Horizon System) Offences Act 2024.
answer_text: My Department is currently working with the UK Government and our operational partners on implementation of the Act. This includes identifying any cases within the scope of the legislation, notifying the individuals concerned, and ensuring their records are updated. This work is ongoing.Although the number of cases that have been identified in Northern Ireland is relatively small, the hurt and distress the Horizon scandal inflicted on the individuals concerned is no less significant. I am, therefore, keen to ensure that all those who have had convictions overturned or cautions deleted are notified as soon as possible, and that those eligible to receive financial redress should be allowed to access this as swiftly as possible.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for an update on actions taken to address the illegal dumping of waste.
answer_text: The Northern Ireland Environment Agency’s Environmental Crime Unit (ECU) is focused on investigating and pursuing offenders involved in the illegal dumping of waste. The ECU focuses its resource on those cases which present the most significant risk to the environment and public health.  The ECU utilises powers contained within the Waste and Contaminated Land (Northern Ireland) Order 1997 to pursue prosecutions which are often coupled with confiscation proceedings under the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 aimed at depriving those convicted of the proceeds generated from their crimes whilst asserting the ‘Polluter Pays Principle’.To address illegal dumping the ECU continues to adopt a multi-agency approach through operational cooperation with other government agencies.  It remains an active member of the Organised Crime Task Force (OCTF) and Joint Unit for Waste Crime (JUWC). These groups bring together law enforcement agencies, along with Government Departments, and other interested parties to combat illegal dumping of waste. By working together with partner agencies, the ECU takes a comprehensive approach to tackle illegal dumping by combining strategic enforcement through effective inter-agency collaboration.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any plans she has to meet representatives of the hospitality industry over the challenges faced in the sector due to rising utility and labour costs outside the industry's control.
answer_text: I have already met with representatives of the hospitality industry on three occasions since taking up office, including a bi-lateral meeting with sector representatives to discuss the issues facing the sector in detail.In relation to the specific issues of rising utility and labour costs, these are matters which go beyond my own immediate Ministerial portfolio, and as cross-cutting matters will need to be considered collectively by the Executive as it sets its strategic priorities and liaises with the British government.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department has a park and ride strategy to ensure optimal access to the rail and rapid transit networks.
To ask the Minister for Infrastructure when construction of the B101 North Lisburn Feeder Road from Thaxton, Lisburn, will commence.
answer_text: The North Lisburn Feeder Road, is a developer led scheme. My officials are working with the developer, the North Lisburn Development Consortium, through an Article 122 Agreement to acquire the final piece of land required to complete the scheme, through the vesting process. Only when the land is vested, which could take up to a year to complete, can construction begin. I cannot therefore put a definitive time on commencement.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on the towpath upgrade from Aghalee to Broadwater, Co. Antrim.
answer_text: Essential reservoir safety works at Broadwater Reservoir are currently at detailed design stage. Construction works are expected to take place during late 2025 to early 2026.An upgrade to the towpath from Aghalee to Broadwater will be considered once the reservoir safety works are completed.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether he plans to review the Home to School Transport policy.
To ask the Minister for the Economy to outline what action he is taking to address the challenges faced by the hospitality sector as a result of rising costs.
To ask the Minister of Education whether there are any plans to increase the amount of toilet facilities in Dromore High School.
answer_text: In November 2023 officials from my Department advised Dromore High School how to submit a minor works application when they raised concerns over toilet provision.  The Education Authority (EA) has confirmed that the school has not yet submitted an application.  I would encourage the school to submit an unavoidable minor works application to the EA.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether his Department will consider introducing a temporary 20mph speed limit at Brookfield Special School, Moira.
answer_text: There have been two tranches of part time 20mph speed limits delivered, which introduced these measures at 215 schools, an investment of about £4m.  A review is underway to determine if a further tranche of the 20mph schools programme is justified. The findings of this review will be made public later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for an update on the progress of developing a Renewable Electricity Support Scheme.
To ask the Minister for Communities what additional funding he has requested to help meet the assessed need identified by the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer.
answer_text: The budget allocated for the Sub-Regional Programme is £36.2m. However, I am aware that local football club facilities are badly in need of investment and that this budget is insufficient. I expect funding to come from a variety of sources, including match funding sources secured by footballing bodies and clubs themselves. I also intend to make a strong case for additional public funding and have already met the UK Sports Minister to discuss the needs of local football.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Communities for an update on the delivery of a National Football Training Centre, as envisaged by the Sub-Regional Stadia Programme for Soccer.
answer_text: I share the frustration of many that a National Training Centre has not been progressed and my officials are working closely with the Irish FA to take forward its development. I look forward to receiving a business case for a National Training Centre from the Irish FA, which demonstrates deliverable benefits and value for money. I will then be able to consider proposals for funding the project.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure how much funding has been allocated in the Lagan Valley constituency during the current financial year for (i) road maintenance; and (ii) payments to motorists for damage caused to vehicles due to road defects.
answer_text: Part (i)My Department does not maintain records of funding by constituency, however details of budget currently allocated to capital Structural Maintenance can be provided for Eastern and Southern Divisions.The capital Structural Maintenance budget is £99m, of which £13.5m has been allocated to Eastern Division and £28.6m to Southern Division. Capital Structural Maintenance includes resurfacing and reconstruction, asphalt patching, surface dressing, capital investment in both drainage infrastructure and in the structural stability of embankments.Resource expenditure on road maintenance includes carriageway and footways patching. The current resource structural maintenance budget for Eastern Division is £2.3m and £5.2m for Southern Division.  Part (ii)I cannot respond to the member’s specific question as my Department does not allocate budgets by Assembly constituency area.I can confirm, however, that the current budget for the 2023-2024 Financial Year for expenditure on all types of road related public liability claims across the North is £7.8m.

answer_text: Part (i) My Department does not maintain records of spend by constituency, however details of expenditure on Structural Maintenance outturns analysed between resource and capital can be provided for the Eastern and Southern Divisions.Table 1: Structural MaintenanceStructural Maintenance£’mEastern Division18-1919-20 20-21 21-2222-23Capital13.312.910.413.914.9Resource2.21.92.62.72.8Total15.514.813.016.617.7Southern DivisionCapital27.426.224.023.529.2Resource5.14.65.15.25.1Total32.530.829.128.734.3Capital Structural Maintenance includes resurfacing and reconstruction, asphalt patching, surface dressing, capital investment in both drainage infrastructure and in the structural stability of embankments.Resource Structural Maintenance includes patching of carriageway and footways.Expenditure is determined by budget availability and capacity in any given year.Part (ii)Records on roads related public liability claims are maintained by Roads Section Office, which broadly align with Council areas.
